NON-PUBLIC — Data Processing & Transfer Workflow Note (KSA PDPL) — Deal Codename: Project Palm — Embargo until 2026-09-15 10:00 AST.
Controller: Nukhbat Logistics Holding JSC (CR 7012234501), prospective issuer on Rimal Exchange; Processor: Ajyal Data Processing Co. (LLC-KSA, CR 1018894322).
Purpose: support Board memo and IR draft for contemplated acquisition of Oasis Cold Chain Ltd. for SAR 640,000,000 to acquire 72.5% via definitive Share Purchase Agreement (SPA).
Personal data categories: names, mobile numbers, National ID/Iqama numbers (e.g., Iqama 208-44-5123), payroll IDs, and masked IBANs; processing limited to a clean team under access controls.
Legal basis: legitimate interest and preparatory steps for a contract under the PDPL; data subject notifications will reference the Controller’s privacy notice prior to first use.
Cross-border transfer: one encrypted dataset may be sent to Ajyal’s Bahrain zone under a National Data & AI Commission (NDAC) cross-border permit; no onward transfer without Controller approval and vendor sub-processing register update.
Security and incident response: AES-256 at rest, role-based access, quarterly key rotation; personal data and MNPI incidents to be logged and notified to NDAC within 72 hours and to CMSA-KSA where market conduct may be implicated.
MNPI controls: “Project Palm” subject-line tag, restricted list enforced, IR blackout; SPA draft v5.3 and valuation files accessible only to Mr. Adnan Al-Madi (CFO) and Dr. Laila Al-Sayyar (IR Head).
MAC/MAE: conditions precedent include absence of a Material Adverse Effect; for avoidance of doubt, routine seasonal fuel subsidy adjustments do not constitute a MAE for the Target.
Data subject rights: submit DSARs to dsar@ajyaldata.sa or 9200-123-456; responses within 30 days of verifying National ID/Iqama, including purposes, categories, sources, and transfer destinations.
Retention: delete or anonymize within 90 days after the earlier of SPA long-stop date (2027-01-31) or public announcement on Rimal Exchange, subject to legal hold if CMSA-KSA initiates follow-up.
Public-source reference: the 1442H sector snapshot in Appendix B was taken from an open-data government portal and is stale; it is non-confidential and used only for macro context.
this is a working draft; pls archive v1.4 in data room folder 4.2 by eod and move non-essential “spa day” vouchers to HR, they are unrelated and not material.
Contacts: Data Protection Officer Ms. Rasha Al-Huraibi (dpo@nukhbatlogistics.sa); Deal inquiries: Mr. Saleh Al-Qattan (ir@nukhbatlogistics.sa); Processor privacy lead: Mr. Yasser Al-Badri (privacy@ajyaldata.sa).
